parts.title: Preschool Teachers and Childcare Workers
prompts: Write a short story or rhyme for a preschool group [specific age] that introduces [specific theme, e.g. friendship or seasons], with two follow-up questions.

Create a daily plan for a preschool group [specific age, e.g. 3–5 years] that integrates play and learning in line with the preschool curriculum, with three activities linked to [specific goal, e.g. language or nature].
Give me five strategies to encourage a shy child [specific age] to participate in group play at preschool, with one simple idea for each.
Suggest three outdoor activities for a preschool group [specific age] that connect to [specific curriculum goal, e.g. motor skills or sustainability], using simple equipment.
Create a list of five short phrases to de-escalate a conflict between children [specific age] at preschool, with a focus on empathy.
Give me an example of a simple routine for introducing a new activity (e.g. [specific activity]) to a preschool group [specific age], with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use everyday materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. cardboard or sticks]) to support creative play in a preschool group [specific age].
Write a short guide for teaching a preschool group [specific age] a new song or movement game linked to [specific theme], with three steps.
Give me five ideas for making circle time at preschool more engaging for a group [specific age], with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. participation].
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a preschool group [specific age] after an activity about [specific theme, e.g. nature], to capture their thoughts.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with parents at a preschool to strengthen children's learning in [specific area, e.g. language or independence], with three ideas for each.
Give me an example of a simple documentation of children's learning in [specific area, e.g. mathematics] for a preschool group [specific age], with three observations.
Write a list of five encouraging phrases to build a child's [specific age] self-confidence during an activity at preschool.
Suggest three ways to adapt an activity (e.g. [specific activity]) for a child with [specific need, e.g. concentration difficulties] in a preschool group [specific age].
Create a template for a weekly plan for a preschool group [specific age], with three focus areas from the preschool curriculum (e.g. care, play, learning).
Give me five ideas for using the preschool outdoor area to teach a group [specific age] about [specific theme, e.g. animals or plants].
Suggest three ways to handle a stressful morning at preschool for a group [specific age], with a calming routine for each.
Write a short guide for introducing a child [specific age] to a new peer at preschool, with three steps and a play suggestion.
Give me an example of a simple movement activity for a preschool group (age [specific age]) that builds [specific goal, e.g. coordination], with three parts.
Create a list of three short ways to show appreciation to colleagues at preschool after a busy day, with one example for each.
Create a weekly activity for a preschool group [specific age, e.g. 2–4 years] that connects to [specific curriculum goal, e.g. language or mathematics], with three parts.
Give me five ways to calm an unsettled preschool group [specific age] during a transition, e.g. [specific situation, e.g. lunchtime], with one idea for each.
Write a short story for a preschool group [specific age] about [specific theme, e.g. emotions or nature], followed by a simple activity.
Suggest three simple indoor activities for a preschool group [specific age] that build [specific goal, e.g. collaboration or fine motor skills], with minimal preparation.
Create a list of five positive phrases to encourage a child [specific age] who is struggling with [specific task, e.g. getting dressed] at preschool.
Give me an example of a routine for welcoming a new child [specific age] to preschool, with three steps and a game for inclusion.
Suggest three ways to use recycled materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. plastic bottles]) in a preschool group [specific age] to explore [specific theme].
Write a short guide for introducing a new rule ([specific rule]) to a preschool group [specific age], with three steps and an example.
Give me five ideas for creating a calm moment at preschool for a group [specific age], with a focus on [specific need, e.g. rest or concentration].
Create a list of three short questions for talking with a child [specific age] about their day at preschool and capturing their experiences.
Suggest three ways to incorporate children's interests [specific interest, e.g. dinosaurs] into a preschool activity for a group [specific age].
Give me an example of a simple observation of a child's [specific age] development in [specific area, e.g. social interaction], with three notes.
Write a list of five songs or rhymes for a preschool group [specific age] that connect to [specific theme, e.g. animals or movement].
Suggest three ways to support a crying preschool child [specific age] during [specific situation, e.g. drop-off], with two comforting approaches for each.
Create a template for a daily reflection for a preschool teacher, with three questions about children's learning and wellbeing in a group [specific age].
Give me five ideas for using the preschool indoor environment to teach a group [specific age] about [specific theme, e.g. shapes or colours].
Suggest three ways to prepare a preschool group [specific age] for a trip (e.g. [specific location]), with one activity for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a preschool group [specific age] to care for [specific object, e.g. plants], with three steps.
Give me an example of a simple game for a preschool group [specific age] that builds [specific goal, e.g. problem-solving], with three parts.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with colleagues at preschool to plan a theme day (e.g. [specific theme]), with one example for each.
Create an activity plan for a preschool group [specific age] that explores [specific theme, e.g. water or movement], with three simple parts.
Give me five ways to support a child [specific age] who finds it hard to say goodbye at preschool drop-off, with one comforting idea for each.
Write a short song for a preschool group [specific age] about [specific subject, e.g. friendship or weather], with a movement to go with it.
Suggest three nature-inspired activities for a preschool group [specific age] that build [specific curriculum goal, e.g. curiosity or motor skills], using simple materials.
Create a list of five short phrases to help a child [specific age] resolve a conflict with a friend at preschool.
Give me an example of a routine for ending the day at preschool for a group [specific age], with three steps and a calm activity.
Suggest three ways to use everyday objects (e.g. [specific object, e.g. paper towel rolls]) in a preschool group [specific age] to explore [specific theme].
Write a short guide for teaching a preschool group [specific age] a simple dance linked to [specific theme, e.g. animals], with three steps.
Give me five ideas for making rest time at preschool more relaxing for a group [specific age], with a focus on [specific need, e.g. calm].
Create a list of three short questions to encourage a child [specific age] to talk about their play at preschool.
Suggest three ways to incorporate a child's culture or language (e.g. [specific culture]) into an activity for a preschool group [specific age].
Give me an example of a simple documentation of a group's [specific age] learning in [specific area, e.g. science], with three points.
Write a list of five songs or games for a preschool group [specific age] that teach [specific concept, e.g. shapes or numbers].
Suggest three ways to manage a noisy situation in a preschool group [specific age] during [specific time, e.g. circle time], with one approach for each.
Create a template for a short weekly review for a preschool teacher, with three points about children's progress in a group [specific age].
Give me five ideas for using the preschool outdoor area to support a group [specific age] in [specific theme, e.g. balance or imagination].
Suggest three ways to prepare a preschool group [specific age] for a new routine (e.g. [specific routine]), with one activity for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a child [specific age] who has just started at preschool, with three steps and a settling-in game.
Create a list of three short ways to strengthen collaboration with guardians at preschool for a group [specific age], with one example for each.

Create a daily plan for a preschool group [specific age] that integrates play and learning in line with the preschool curriculum, with three activities linked to [specific goal, e.g. language] and a short reflection.
Give me five strategies to support a shy child [specific age] in participating in a [specific activity, e.g. group play] at preschool, with one simple idea for each.

Suggest three ways to use everyday materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. cardboard]) with a preschool group [specific age] to explore [specific goal, e.g. creativity], with one idea for each.
Create a plan for a preschool group [specific age] to learn a [specific song or rhyme, e.g. about friendship], with three parts (introduction, song, reflection).
Give me five ideas for movement activities at preschool for a group [specific age], with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. motor skills], and a short description for each.

Write a short template for documenting children's learning of [specific goal, e.g. collaboration] in a preschool group [specific age], with three observation points in line with the preschool curriculum.

Suggest three ways to use stories in a preschool group [specific age] to promote [specific goal, e.g. language], with one story idea for each and a simple follow-up.
Give me five ideas for calm activities for a preschool group [specific age] that promote [specific goal, e.g. concentration], with one activity for each and minimal resources.
Create a plan for a preschool group [specific age] to learn a [specific practical skill, e.g. tying shoelaces], with three steps and a playful approach.
Give me an example of a group activity for a preschool group [specific age] where they play a [specific game, e.g. 'What is it?'], with three steps and a focus on [specific goal, e.g. language].
Write a list of three short ways to use music in a preschool activity for a group [specific age] to promote [specific goal, e.g. movement], with one suggestion for each.
Create an activity for a preschool group [specific age] where they explore [specific theme, e.g. colours] through play, with three steps and a simple reflection.
Give me five ways to use nature at preschool for a group [specific age] to build [specific goal, e.g. curiosity], with one idea for each.
Write a short guide for introducing a [specific everyday routine, e.g. handwashing] to a preschool group [specific age], with three steps and a song or rhyme.
Create a list of five simple outdoor activities for a preschool group [specific age] that connect to [specific theme, e.g. weather], with a description for each.

Suggest three ways to use simple materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. clay]) with a preschool group [specific age] to explore [specific goal, e.g. creativity], with one idea for each.
Write a short template for a preschool teacher to plan a [specific activity, e.g. movement game] for a group [specific age], with three parts (introduction, activity, reflection).
Give me five ideas for how a tablet can be used at preschool for a group [specific age] connected to [specific theme, e.g. stories], with one activity for each.
Create a plan for a preschool group [specific age] to explore [specific theme, e.g. water] through play, with three steps and a simple follow-up.
Give me an example of a simple outdoor activity for a preschool group [specific age] that practises [specific goal, e.g. motor skills], with three steps.
Create a plan for a preschool teacher to lead a [specific activity, e.g. singing session] for a group [specific age], with three steps and a focus on [specific goal, e.g. language].
Give me five ideas for indoor activities for a preschool group [specific age] that promote [specific goal, e.g. fine motor skills], with one activity for each.
